title: Van-tieghemia montana
intro: Van-tieghemia montana, also known as Puna tree or Māmane, is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, native to Hawaii. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Van-tieghemia montana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Van-tieghemia montana is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their potential to promote skin health and wound healing.

The classification of Van-tieghemia montana is further refined to the family level, where it is placed among other flowering plants like the blueberries and rhododendrons.
